compositional_truth_check.findings: P1 (Yellow Pot) contains String of Nickels. P2/P4 (Black Pot, bottom) shows a single, isolated sprout/leaf structure. P3 (Black Pot, top) contains the Pothos and the White Rabbit anchor. No 'Systemic Loss' of pots, but significant biomass reduction in P2/P4.

plant_audit.P2_P4_Combined: Severe decline. Only one primary leaf structure remains visible in the lower black pot; the rest of the soil is bare.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos shows one large, yellowing/senescing leaf and one healthy green leaf. Rabbit anchor remains stable.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistent dampness observed across all pots; no surface cracking.
biome_observations.debris: White perlite/vermiculite visible on soil surfaces; eggshell fragments noted in P2/P4.

temporal_deltas.T_earliest_to_T_minus_4: Minimal change; Pothos leaf yellowing begins.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_4_to_T_minus_3: Pothos leaf yellowing intensifies; P2/P4 sprout remains static.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_2_to_T_minus_1: Pothos leaf shows increased chlorosis; P2/P4 sprout appears slightly more translucent.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_1_to_current: Current state shows continued senescence in P3 and stagnation in P2/P4.

visual_health_inference.P3: Stressed (Nutrient deficiency or overwatering indicated by yellowing)

anomalies.intervention: Eggshell fragments in P2/P4 suggest an attempted soil amendment or calcium supplementation.
anomalies.growth: No incidental weeds or secondary sprouts detected.

vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of low-vigor. The Pothos (P3) is exhibiting signs of chlorosis, likely due to root stress or light-to-water imbalance. The P2/P4 container has suffered a near-total loss of the expected specimens, leaving only a single, fragile sprout. The environment is stable but requires immediate intervention to prevent further decline of the Pothos and to assess the viability of the remaining sprout in the lower pot.
